channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: Marwan Barghouti’s son calls for his release on stage at Berlin concert
item.description: At Berlin’s Citadel Music Festival, activist Arab Barghouti called for the release of his father.

item.title: Israel intensifies strikes in southern Lebanon killing 12 people
item.description: Al Jazeera’s Obaida Hitto reports on the strikes he witnessed in and around Tyre.

item.title: Trump warns Netanyahu: ‘You’ll be on your own’ if attacks on Iran continue
item.description: Trump's warning comes as Israel and Iran pause attacks and indirect talks to reach a peace deal continue.

item.title: US lists China’s BYD, Alibaba, Baidu as ‘Chinese military companies’
item.description: Chinese embassy in Washington, DC, condemns designation, calling it 'discriminatory'.

item.title: Botswana diamond slump hits miners living on the edge of survival
item.description: Botswana’s diamond-driven economy is under mounting strain as global demand weakens and production falls.

item.title: New York City crowd boos US President Donald Trump at NBA playoff game
item.description: US President Donald Trump was loudly booed when he was shown on screens before Game 3 of the NBA Finals.

item.title: UN questions legality of Israeli forced evacuation orders in Lebanon
item.description: A UN spokesman says forced evacuation orders issued across southern and eastern Lebanon are nearly impossible to follow

item.title: ICC prosecutor suspended pending vote on sexual misconduct claims
item.description: Karim Khan rejects suspension as ICC governing body refers him to disciplinary proceedings before member states.

item.title: Israel continues strikes on Lebanon despite halting attacks on Iran
item.description: Israeli PM Benjamin Netanyahu acknowledged a halt in fighting with Iran but will continue operations against Hezbollah.

item.title: Iran war live: Trump warns Netanyahu as Israel, Tehran halt fighting
item.description: Israeli strikes have killed 3,637 people in Lebanon since March, with 11,188 wounded, the Health Ministry says.

item.title: Maine’s Platner faces test as four US states hold midterm primary votes
item.description: Four states - Maine, Nevada, South Carolina and North Dakota - are holding primaries ahead of November's midterms.

item.title: Trump nominates Todd Blanche as US attorney general, teeing up Senate fight
item.description: Blanche, who currently serves as acting attorney general, has faced controversy over the Epstein files and January 6.

item.title: Survivors recall Israeli raid that killed 274 in Gaza refugee camp
item.description: Two years on, witnesses recount the June 2024 Israeli raid on Nuseirat refugee camp to free four captives.

item.title: Tech giant OpenAI files for US initial public offering
item.description: OpenAI did not disclose the size or terms of the offering and said a timeline has not yet been determined.

item.title: UN human rights leader calls for Cuba sanctions to be ‘lifted immediately’
item.description: Volker Turk, the UN's high commissioner for human rights, warns that 'children are dying' as result of US oil blockade.

item.title: US confirms it denied entry to Somali referee set to take part in World Cup
item.description: Award-winning referee Omar Artan was 'determined to be inadmissible due to vetting concerns' after arriving in Miami.

item.title: Pope Leo makes visit to Spain’s Almudena Cathedral
item.description: Crowds in Madrid were delighted to see Pope Leo make a visit to Santa Maria de la Almudena Cathedral with Queen Sofia.

item.title: EU planning to add to $1.5 trillion sanctions hit on Russia
item.description: New proposed listings target Russia's 'military industrial complex, human rights violators and propagandists'.

item.title: Italian prosecutors to probe Israeli minister Ben-Gvir over flotilla abuse
item.description: Rome is the second in Europe to launch a probe into Israel's treatment of activists detained as they headed to Gaza.

item.title: Could Iran and the US play each other at the World Cup?
item.description: Could Iran and the US play each other at the World Cup?

item.title: ‘We basically lost’: Tel Aviv residents react after Israel-Iran strikes
item.description: Residents in Tel Aviv voiced mixed reactions after a day of missile exchanges between Israel and Iran.

item.title: Gaza cancer patient waits for a chance at life
item.description: A school director and novelist in Gaza needs urgent life-saving cancer surgery that she cannot access inside the Strip.

item.title: How significant is Xi Jinping’s visit to North Korea?
item.description: Beijing is Pyongyang's main political and economic backer.

item.title: France and Germany scrap fighter jet in hit to European defence cooperation
item.description: The termination of the plan comes as Washington increases pressure on Europe to become more militarily independent.

item.title: Will the FIFA World Cup be the economic bonanza US cities were promised?
item.description: Cities remain optimistic despite lagging hotel bookings and high ticket prices that might deter foreign travellers.
